report_name: EU Proposes 'Tech Sovereignty Package' to Boost Independence in Cloud, AI, and Chips

identities.name: European Commission
identities.description: The executive branch of the European Union, which proposed the 'Tech Sovereignty Package' on June 3, 2026, to enhance the EU's digital independence.

identities.name: European Union
identities.description: A political and economic union whose digital independence and competitiveness are the focus of the 'Tech Sovereignty Package'.

identities.name: Non-EU Technology Companies
identities.description: A class of companies, including US cloud providers, that will be affected by the new regulations and will need to navigate the CADA security and sovereignty framework.

identities.name: EU Technology Companies
identities.description: A class of domestic companies within the EU expected to benefit from the 'Tech Sovereignty Package' through increased funding and streamlined regulations.

identities.name: Critical Sector Organizations
identities.description: Industries such as finance, healthcare, and energy that will be impacted by the new security standards of the Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA).

identities.name: ENISA
identities.description: The European Union Agency for Cybersecurity, mentioned as a potential EU-level body for enforcing the new regulations.
